title: Digital wireless microphones and the RF spectrum squeeze
paras: Analogue UHF radio mics have been squeezed by the ‘digital dividend’: the 700 MHz and 600 MHz bands were reallocated to mobile networks, so those old frequencies are now off-limits. Current systems are digital wireless (Sennheiser EW-DX, Shure Axient Digital, Wisycom), offering encryption, more channels per MHz and cleaner audio than analogue companding.

title: 32-bit float recording and timecode sync
paras: Field recorders (Sound Devices MixPre, Zoom F-series) now record in 32-bit float. The dynamic range is so wide that clipping and under-recording are effectively impossible — levels are set afterwards in post. Timecode generators (Tentacle Sync, UltraSync) keep multi-camera and separate sound recorders locked together.

title: Audio over IP (Dante, AES67) and immersive sound
paras: Analogue multicore and MADI are giving way to audio-over-IP: Dante and the open AES67 standard carry hundreds of channels over ordinary Ethernet with sample-accurate sync. Delivery has also moved towards immersive formats — Dolby Atmos for cinema and streaming, and ambisonics for 360°/VR.

title: DAT, MiniDisc and the old UHF bands
paras: DAT and MiniDisc as recording media, and analogue radio mics operating in the vacated 700/800 MHz bands, are obsolete — transmitting in those frequencies is now illegal in most countries.
